ScopeThe methodThe five stepsWhat we cannot account forWhere to look
/markings/err/
← All markings

ERR stamp

The mark of Rosenberg’s task force, which looted Jewish libraries to stock a planned institute in Frankfurt. Over a dozen sub-variants exist; the form seen in these books is “Sichergestellt durch Einsatzstab RR Reval” — secured by the ERR at Reval (Tallinn).

Examples, photographed in the book
clean example — ERR stamp
partly obscured — ink-cancelled after the war

One of more than a dozen distinct Nazi confiscation stamps recorded in these books; the ERR competed with the RSHA for the same libraries.

Issuing body
Einsatzstab Reichsleiter Rosenberg (ERR)
Where it appears
Title page and front pastedown
See on the diagram →
In Penn holdings
4 books
Reference
Bencowitz, Library Markings (1946), NLI
Books carrying this marking →
Provenance research & text: Bruce Nielsen · Penn Libraries, Katz Center for Advanced Judaic StudiesUncertainty in this record is shown, not hidden.
